DSP Architecture Simulation

SYSTOLIC ARRAY
FIR FILTER

Weight-stationary 1-D systolic array implementing a digital FIR filter.
Same fundamental architecture as Google's TPU — simulated clock-cycle accurate in Python.

16FIR Taps / PEs
2.22e-16Max Numerical Error
1 kHzSample Rate

systolic_array.py + dsp_signals.py + visualizer.py → run_all.py encodes outputs as base64 → index.html (self-contained, no server needed)

// architecture

What is a Systolic Array?

⚙️

Processing Elements (PEs)

Each PE holds one FIR coefficient. Every clock it multiplies its weight by the incoming sample and accumulates the partial sum from the left.

multiply-accumulate
🔁

Weight-Stationary

Coefficients are loaded once and stay fixed. Only data (samples) moves through a shift register — each PE sees a delayed version of the input.

data flows, weights stay
⚡

True Parallelism

Every PE fires simultaneously every clock cycle. No central controller, no memory bus. At steady state — 100% PE utilization, every single cycle.

all PEs fire each clock
🖥️

TPU Connection

Google's TPU v1 uses a 256×256 systolic array (65,536 PEs) for matrix multiply. Our 1-D array is the same principle applied to digital filtering.

same as Google TPU
DATA FLOW — samples shift right each clock · partial sums accumulate →
x[n] →
PE 0h[0]
→
PE 1h[1]
→
PE 2h[2]
→
···
→
PE N-1h[N-1]
→ y[n]

Each PE computes: partial_sum_out = partial_sum_in + h[i] × delay_line[i]

Accuracy & Results

MetricValueStatusNotes
Max Absolute Error vs NumPy2.22e-16✓ PASS ✓Near float64 machine epsilon
SNR Before Filtering11.9 dBInputNoisy 50 Hz sinusoid
SNR After Filtering-5.3 dB✓ ImprovedAfter 16-tap Hamming LPF
SNR Improvement+-17.3 dB✓ MeasuredComputed by backend, injected into HTML
PE utilization (steady state)100%✓ OptimalAll PEs fire every clock after pipeline fill
Filters verified5 designs✓ All passLPF 8/16/32-tap, BPF, HPF
Signal types available6 types✓ LoadedSinusoid, multi-tone, chirp, ECG, step, impulse
